We recommended that Management install adequate internal controls over cash management that are consistent with laws, existing rules and regulations, especially in its collection and deposit procedures requiring intact remittance of collection daily, to ensure that government funds are adequately safeguarded from misuse, misappropriation, irregularities and theft.

Further, we recommended that the provisions of Joint Circular No. 2015-01 should be strictly observed to ensure the proper handling and application of the fund.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant refrain from recording expenses under the “Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses” account for which specific accounts have been provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGU’s as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, thereby fairly present the actual expenditures under their proper accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly monitor the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances through compliance with the regulations as provided in COA Circular 97-002 and PD 1445. We also recommend that demand letters be sent to those with outstanding cash advances and require its immediate liquidation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit all the required documents necessary to establish the validity, legality and propriety of the amount paid for the honoraria of members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), Technical Working Group and BAC Secretariat amounting to P1,417,903.50 paid for the year 2019, and henceforth, disburse only the claims for said incentive with complete documentation.

We recommend management thru the Municipal Accountant to submit all the required documents necessary to establish the validity, legality and propriety of the amount paid for the Calamity assistance amounting to P3,080,000.00 suspended in audit, and henceforth, disburse only the claims against government funds with complete documentation.

We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of IRR of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF on or before 15th day after the end of each month as prescribed in Sec. 5.1.5 of the same Circular to facilitate compliance of the Municipality on the proper accounting and reporting of the fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to submit a copy of the DILG-approved annual GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit Team assigned to the agency within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 to prove the Municipality’s response to gender-related issues and concerns in the locality.

We recommend that management to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 on the submission of accounts and other reports of the municipality on or before the set deadline, to avoid administrative sanctions and/or disallowance of said accounts, as provided for in Section 39 (1) & (3) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from the different honoraria paid by the Municipality and the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period, and enjoined to abide by the revised tax rates provided in R.A. 10963 or the Tax Reform for Acceleration & Inclusion (TRAIN).

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exercise the diligence of a good father of a family in supervising accountable officers under her control as well as accountable forms and reports to prevent the incurrence of loss of government funds and property in pursuance of Section 69, 104 and 107 of P.D. 1445 and Item B of Section 60 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual to avoid becoming jointly and severally liable together with the erring collectors and collection clerks designated as collectors.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned accountable officers to immediately produce all accountable forms in their custody for proper accounting and reporting. Also, we recommended strict enforcement for the immediate settlement of all their unremitted collections. Otherwise, appropriate charges shall be imposed for failure to render accounts.

We recommended that management direct the disbursing officer to immediately liquidate, return and/or refund unclaimed salaries, wages and etc. to the Municipal Treasurer. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to stop the grant of cash advance for salaries, wages and honoraria when the previous cash advance has not been liquidated and/or fully refunded pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. 

We also recommended that management reduce the monthly cash advances granted to the Disbursing Officer by way of adopting a system of payroll payment through the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) of Government Bank or paying directly to payees thru checks for disbursements with material amounts, thereby strengthening the internal control on cash management.

Initiate corrective measures to fast-track the implementation and completion of unimplemented and ongoing priority projects for prior years and 2018 in order to avoid accumulation of unimplemented projects and to ensure the optimal utilization of 20% development fund in the ensuing year at lower cost due to soaring prices of commodities.
